concept: Brave / Courageous / Facing Fear with Resolve
description: Ready to face and endure danger or pain; showing courage. 'Shaelo' names the quality of courage—not fearlessness but the willingness to act rightly despite fear.
sound_symbolism: The word faces forward. 'Sha' /ʃa/ begins with breath released, fear acknowledged. The vowel 'e' opens to what must be faced. Then 'lo' moves forward with steady resolve, courage embodied.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'shaelo miona' (brave person). Phi courage is not aggressive—it faces difficulty without causing harm, protects without attacking.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: True courage faces suffering directly—the bravery to see things as they are and act from compassion despite difficulty.
pillars.solarpunk-values: Building sustainable futures requires courage—the willingness to change despite uncertainty and resist harmful systems.
